definition: CalibrationStage-derived perf_score concentrates havoc mutations on small/fast/stable seeds to assemble deep, structurally-valid inputs (multi-field formats, long expressions, chained parse paths) whose complexity naive's flat mutation budget rarely achieves within the campaign budget.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage assigns higher havoc budgets to small/fast/stable structured seeds, giving minimizer far more mutation attempts to assemble the deep DOCTYPE path that reaches this pointer-equality check.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy concentrates havoc on stable seeds to reach deep pointer-equality gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Resolving the name3 pointer-identity check requires a deeply-nested DTD that interns and re-looks-up the same name3 string so dict pointers alias.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives the parser deep enough into the DTD entity/element-decl path that xmlHashLookup3 is reached with a dict-interned name3 equal (by pointer) to a stored entry's name3; reaching it at all needs a large, well-formed DOCTYPE structure that survives many parse stages.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's calibration-derived energy gives faster/smaller/more-stable seeds more havoc mutations per visit, so the FIFO corpus of structured DTD seeds is mutated enough to assemble the deep DOCTYPE that reaches and resolves the lookup (resolved 9/10, hitcount 36).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs the same havoc+token stack in a plain StdMutationalStage with no calibrated energy, so it never accumulates the mutation budget on the structured seeds and stays blocked 10/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage feeds a perf_score-derived per-seed mutation budget (StdPowerMutationalStage) that naive lacks; this calibrated energy is the single-technique delta enabling the winner.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a pointer-identity comparison of name3 inside xmlHashLookup3 on the dict path.
The comparison sits on the dict-interned branch, where equality is pointer identity established by dict interning.
The branch is reached via deep DTD parsing (DOCTYPE / entity / attribute-list-decl call chain).
minimizer beats naive on the calibrated_energy delta, resolving 9/10 vs naive 0/10.
minimizer derives per-seed mutation energy from intrinsic calibration which naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit

evidence_trail.claim: naive runs the havoc+token stack in a plain StdMutationalStage with no I2S and no calibrated energy.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: fast is identical to minimizer except the FAST AFLFast power schedule.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore `fast`
vs `minimizer`, not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: FAST schedule damps saturated paths to 0.4x energy via the n_fuzz factor.
minimizer beats fast on the aflfast_rarity delta (9/10 vs 2/10).
grimoire beats cmplog on the grimoire_structural delta, resolving 10/10 with hitcount 1004.
grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage plus structural Grimoire mutators on top of the cmplog stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog uses only havoc+token plus I2S, with no structural recombination.
value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile on the I2S delta (8/10 vs 8 blocked).
I2SRandReplace scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator.
value_profile_cmplog beats plain cmplog on the value_profile delta (8/10 vs 2/10).
The CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets and preserves inputs that bring operands closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The winning grimoire seed contains a complete DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtd reference structure verbatim.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness isolating the name3 dict-interned identity check where value_profile_cmplog and value_profile resolve at equal rates (no I2S advantage), and where grimoire shows no structural advantage over cmplog, would refute the multi-feature attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The I2S hypothesis is unverified in lineage because no vpc winning seed was bisected for this branch, so the I2S-vs-CMP_MAP split between Pairs 4 and 5 rests on mechanism reasoning rather than seed-byte evidence.

signature.mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage-derived perf_score concentrates havoc mutations on compact well-formed XML seeds, raising the probability of completing the entity-ref structural path without any comparison introspection.
signature.one_line: calibrated power schedule focuses havoc on small well-formed seeds to resolve entity-ref path

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed XML document whose body contains an entity reference '&name;' to an undeclared entity, keeping ctxt->wellFormed==1 and ent==NULL at the blocker

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural requirement; no comparison-solving aid, so the win comes from spending more mutation energy on the small well-formed XML seeds that already carry most of the prolog/markup.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's calibration-derived power schedule gives faster, smaller, more-stable seeds more havoc mutations per visit, so the compact valid-XML seeds (e.g. the 257-258 byte ones) get heavily mutated and complete the entity-ref path, resolving 8/10.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs the same havoc/token stack under a plain mutational stage with no energy weighting, spreading mutations uniformly and leaving the structural tokens unrepaired; it stays blocked 8/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's calibrated energy (StdPowerMutationalStage with calibration-based perf_score, PowerSchedule None) concentrates havoc effort on small/stable well-formed seeds, raising the chance of completing the valid-XML structure without any CMP introspection.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the well-formed-and-undeclared-entity test inside xmlParseEntityRef; the globally blocked side is the true branch.
Globally blocked side is the true branch.

The winner seed continues with valid version and DOCTYPE SYSTEM markup.
Loser seeds carry corrupting binary/garbage prefixes ahead of the prolog.
Another loser seed begins with non-XML ascii junk before the prolog.
On the blocker line the winner takes the true side while the loser takes only the false side many more times.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the same well-formed-prolog-then-undeclared-entity structure where cmplog does NOT outpace naive in reaching the gated branch (no I2S advantage), refuting the claim that the literal XML structural tokens are the comparison-solvable feature driving the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact comparison(s) that gate well-formedness are inferred from the prolog/DOCTYPE bytes in the seeds and source, not directly observed as named CMP operands in the prompt, so the precise I2S-solved literal is uncertain.

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy (CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage) awards more havoc mutations to the small, fast, stable structured-header seeds, concentrating effort until the longer TE-enabling input is assembled and stabilized.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy concentrates havoc on stable structured seeds to build TE-enabling input

analysis.summary_one_line: A long, structured request that turns on transfer-encoding (no user TE header) so the false branch enters the TE block.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A larger (~110-122 byte) input carrying a stable, well-formed header structure (the 0x06-0x1f 'pp@3&8Mnn' run plus a 'Gte;' / 'TE' token region) that sets data->set.http_transfer_encoding while NOT supplying a user 'TE' header, so the && at 3051 takes the false side and enters the TE block.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds are 110-122 bytes, share the identical structured prefix run '00 01 00 00 00 19 70 70 40 33 26 38 4d 6e 6e' and contain '47 74 65' ('Gte') near offset 0x24, exercising the false branch (T=0 F=3 at L3051); the minimizer's calibration burst awards more havoc energy to these faster/smaller/stable corpus entries, letting it preserve and extend the structured prefix.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All naive loser-blocking seeds are only 35-36 bytes and degenerate into '%'/'A'/'-' filler with no 'Gte'/'TE' token region; they take the true branch (T=15 F=0 at L3051), so naive never assembles the longer structured input that toggles http_transfer_encoding without a TE header.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer differs from naive only by the CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage (calibrated energy): perf_score derived from execution time/edge-fill/stability gives more havoc mutations to the structured corpus entries, so the same havoc/token mutators reach and stabilize the longer TE-enabling input that plain naive's flat StdMutationalStage budget does not.

evidence_trail.claim: The single decisive pair is minimizer over naive with technique delta calibrated_energy.
The blocker is the && gate at http.c:3051 whose false side is globally blocked.
Entering the false-branch block depends on data->set.http_transfer_encoding being set, the second && operand.
Winner seeds are 110-122 bytes and share an identical structured prefix run.
Winner seeds contain a 'Gte' token region near offset 0x24.
Loser seeds are only 35-36 bytes of degenerate filler.
At the blocker line the winner takes the false branch while the loser stays on the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L3051  T=0 F=3  T=15 F=0  if(!Curl_checkheaders(data, STRCONST("TE")) &&  <-- BLOCKER

the energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often

evidence_trail.claim: Minimizer runs havoc + token mutators inside a StdPowerMutationalStage rather than naive's plain StdMutationalStage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
run inside a `StdPowerMutationalStage` rather than naive's
plain `StdMutationalStage`

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where minimizer and naive resolve the TE false branch at statistically equal rates once both are given the same per-seed mutation budget, showing the calibrated energy schedule (not seed structure) was irrelevant.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seeds do not literally contain a 'TE:' HTTP header, so the byte path that sets data->set.http_transfer_encoding without a user TE header is inferred from the structured prefix rather than directly observed.

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy (CalibrationStage perf_score driving StdPowerMutationalStage) concentrates more havoc mutations on compact fast stable GPOS-bearing font seeds, reaching the attachment-flag gate faster than naive's flat scheduling.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy amplifies mutation effort on compact GPOS seeds to set attachment flag

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a font whose GPOS table sets HB_BUFFER_SCRATCH_FLAG_HAS_GPOS_ATTACHMENT, i.e. drives shaping deep enough to produce a glyph attachment.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same GPOS-attachment-flag-setting font; here the winning edge is reaching it faster by spending many more mutations on the small, stable kern/GPOS seeds that already approach the structure.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer resolves 9/10 with the highest hitcount (208) and the shortest blocked duration (7.30h); its calibration-derived power schedule grants more havoc mutations to faster/smaller/more-stable seeds, concentrating effort on the compact font seeds that are near the attachment-triggering structure.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 (15 hits, 21.80h) because its plain StdMutationalStage spends uniform energy per seed and never amplifies the promising compact font seeds, so it explores the structural neighborhood far more slowly.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's calibrated energy (CalibrationStage perf_score driving a StdPowerMutationalStage with no rarity weighting) reallocates mutation budget toward the stable seeds nearest the GPOS structure, beating naive's flat scheduling.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch is a GPOS-attachment scratch-flag test, a deep semantic state rather than a single CMP magic value.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact comparison operands that value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient ratchets toward are not directly observed; the BYTE DIFF shows differing tag bytes (0x0c-0x0f) but the link from those bytes to the attachment-flag-setting GPOS parse is inferred from the call chain rather than a single decisive CMP.

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy (perf_score from speed/size/stability, PowerSchedule None) gives small fast table-bearing font seeds disproportionate havoc iterations, compounding their structured byte content until the valid GPOS table_index==1 path is reached.

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a valid GPOS table (table_index==1) so init() sets ignore_zwnj, reaching deep GSUB/GPOS layout-matching code.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same GPOS-table structural requirement; the winner reaches it because the small, fast, structured table-bearing seeds earn extra mutation energy that compounds the structured byte content.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 41433 vs loser 2810; its calibration-derived power schedule grants faster/smaller/more-stable seeds more havoc mutations, and three of the winner-resolving seeds (e.g. the 141-byte and 152-byte seeds bearing 'kern'/font-table bytes) are minimizer finds.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs the same havoc/token stack inside a plain StdMutationalStage with a fixed mutation budget per seed visit, so it cannot concentrate effort on the promising small structured seeds and blocks 8/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage derive per-seed energy from intrinsic perf_score (speed/size/stability, PowerSchedule None); the structured table-bearing seeds, being small and fast, earn extra havoc iterations that more often complete the valid table directory, unlike naive's flat budget.

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy assigns disproportionate mutation budget to compact fast stable valid-header seeds, enabling them to approach the variable-font structural gate (table_index==1 and num_coords non-zero) where naive's flat energy stalls.

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy (CalibrationStage perf_score, no rarity weighting) grants small fast near-valid font seeds extra havoc mutations per visit, completing the kern-table structure that flips hb_ot_layout_has_machine_kerning to false.

analysis.summary_one_line: Font must carry a structurally valid kern table that reaches has_machine_kerning returning false, not all-0x20 padding.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A compact, fast-executing font seed whose kern-table fields are concrete enough to reach the has_machine_kerning evaluation; the calibrated power schedule then spends extra mutation energy on such seeds to complete the valid structure.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's winning seeds are small (44-66 bytes) with concrete table bytes (e.g. F2020 28f6 6b65726e 'kern' header at 0x06-0x0f); calibrated energy gives faster/smaller/stabler seeds more havoc mutations per visit, concentrating effort on the near-valid font and reaching the false side fastest (avg blocked 2.90h vs loser 15.40h).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs a plain mutational stage with uniform energy, so it dilutes effort across many 0x20-padded seeds that never form a valid kern table; it stays blocked 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage derives per-seed energy from a calibration perf_score (no rarity weighting), so small fast near-valid font seeds earn disproportionate havoc mutations that complete the kern-table structure flipping the branch false.

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy concentrates havoc mutations on fast small stable SELECT-expression seeds; the extra mutation budget on those seeds raises the chance havoc produces a long well-formed expression that walks deep enough to accumulate a resolver error and set pParse->nErr.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy focuses mutations on SELECT-expression seeds to reach resolver error path

analysis.summary_one_line: A parseable SQL expression that triggers a resolver error (pParse->nErr set), reaching the WRC_Abort true side of resolveExprStep's exit ternary.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SQL statement that parses far enough to call resolveExprStep on an expression tree AND raises a name/function resolution error so pParse->nErr becomes non-zero by line 98693 (e.g. an arithmetic SELECT over unresolved identifiers or a 'no such function' call), driving the ternary's true (WRC_Abort) side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner seed is a 482-byte 'SELECT   - 11111+++1++1,...' arithmetic expression; minimizer resolves the branch 8/10 with avg hitcount 4 and the true side observed (W shows T=1 at L98693), consistent with a deeply-walked expression that accumulates a resolver error.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 (blocked 9), avg hitcount 0, and W/L counts show the loser only ever takes the false side (T=0 F=1870 at L98693); naive's plain mutation budget rarely produces the long well-formed expression needed to both reach the walk and set nErr.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer adds a CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage so faster/smaller/more-stable seeds earn more havoc mutations (calibrated energy); this concentrates mutation budget on the SELECT-expression seeds that walk deep enough to set pParse->nErr, whereas naive's flat StdMutationalStage spreads the same havoc operators thinly and rarely lands the error-raising expression.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true (WRC_Abort) side of a ternary gated on pParse->nErr or mallocFailed.
The globally blocked side is the true branch.
The winner-resolving seed is a long arithmetic SELECT expression.
minimizer resolves 8/10 while naive resolves 1/10 at this branch with the divergence attributed to calibrated_energy.
On the loser the blocker's true side is never taken (T=0) while on the winner it is taken (T=1).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=1 F=111  T=0 F=1870  return (pParse->nErr || pParse->db->mallocFailed) ? WRC_A

evidence_trail.claim: The winner has nonzero avg hitcount on the branch while the loser has zero.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=4  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer derives per-seed havoc count (energy) from a calibration-based perf_score favoring faster/smaller/more-stable seeds.
naive uses a plain StdMutationalStage (no calibrated energy) so havoc budget is not seed-weighted.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
run inside a `StdPowerMutationalStage` rather than naive's
plain `StdMutationalStage`

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where minimizer and naive resolve the WRC_Abort side at equal rates once both are given the same per-seed mutation budget, showing the divergence is not driven by calibrated energy.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the winner's specific advantage is reaching an nErr-setting expression rather than merely a generic depth/throughput effect is inferred from source + one winner seed, not directly measured against the loser's expression structure.

signature.mechanism_summary: Calibrated energy (CalibrationStage perf_score) grants faster smaller more-stable SELECT seeds more havoc mutations per visit, raising the chance havoc assembles the required integer-literal arithmetic body that reaches codeInteger's true branch.
signature.one_line: calibrated energy amplifies havoc on small SELECT seeds to reach integer codeInteger branch

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching codeInteger's true branch needs a SELECT with integer-literal arithmetic expressions, not a trivial SELECT 1; constant.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same integer-literal SELECT-expression feature reaching codeInteger's true branch, found by spending more havoc mutations on small/fast/stable SELECT seeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer resolved 8/10 with avg hitcount 69 on the branch versus the loser's 1, reaching the codegen path far more often.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive's plain StdMutationalStage gives the same uniform energy to every seed and stays blocked 8/10 with hitcount 1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer runs the same havoc+token stack inside a StdPowerMutationalStage whose CalibrationStage-derived perf_score grants faster, smaller, more-stable SELECT seeds more mutations per visit, raising the chance havoc assembles the required literal-arithmetic body.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of codeInteger, SQLite's integer-literal codegen.
The blocked side is the true branch.
cmplog winners take the true branch at the blocker while naive losers do not.
cmplog winning seeds are SELECT bodies densely packed with '5','+','/','-' literal/operator bytes.
naive loser seeds are short trivial SELECT 1; inputs.
cmplog beats naive on this branch via I2S, prob_div 0.80.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile beats naive on this branch via the value_profile technique.
value_profile reaches the branch with avg hitcount 112 vs the loser's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=112  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP preserves inputs that bring a CMP operand pair closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer beats naive on this branch via calibrated_energy.
minimizer reaches the branch with avg hitcount 69 vs the loser's 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=69  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer's power stage gives faster, smaller, more-stable seeds more mutations via calibration perf_score.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: naive applies a uniform plain StdMutationalStage, unlike minimizer's StdPowerMutationalStage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
run inside a `StdPowerMutationalStage` rather than naive's
plain `StdMutationalStage`

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog still fails to take codeInteger's true branch when the integer literal it codegens is replaced by a single fixed constant with no comparison against input bytes (no CMP for I2S to mine), while value_profile/minimizer also fail — showing the divergence is unrelated to integer-literal CMP introspection or energy.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The value_profile and minimizer mechanism attributions rest on hitcount/trial-vector divergence and generic technique knowledge rather than a direct byte-level CMP_MAP or per-seed energy trace at this specific branch.
